Metric: Software name? (string)
URL? (URL)
Affiliation (institution(s)) (string or N/A)
Response: University of Almería (Spain); lead developer: Jose Luis Blanco-Claraco (Full Professor at University of Almería, formerly TU Munich and University of Málaga); founded 2005 by Jose Luis Blanco

Metric: Software purpose (string)
Response: Open-source cross-platform C++ library for mobile robotics research and development; provides algorithms for SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping), computer vision, motion planning, and sensor data processing; includes a 3D robotics simulation environment (RawLogViewer, SceneViewer3D); supports mapping, localization, path planning, and sensor fusion

Platforms? (set of Windows, Linux, OS X, Android, other∗) ∗ given via string

Software Category? The concept category includes software that does not have an officially released version. Public software has a released version in the public domain. Private software has a released version available to authorized users only. (concept, public, private)
Development model? (open source, freeware, commercial, unclear)
Publications about the software? Refers to publications that have used or mentioned the software. (number or unknown)
Source code URL? (set of url, n/a, unclear)
Programming language(s)? (set of FORTRAN, Matlab, C, C++, Java, R, Ruby, Python, Cython, BASIC, Pascal, IDL, unclear, other∗) ∗ given via string
Is there evidence that performance was considered? Performance refers to either speed, storage, or throughput. (yes∗, no)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: MRPT is one of the most comprehensive single-library robotics toolkits: 100+ SLAM algorithms, 3D visualization, sensor simulation, and mobile robot-specific utilities. Includes mrpt-slam, mrpt-nav, mrpt-vision, mrpt-maps modules. Python bindings (pymrpt) available via pip install pymrpt. ROS 2 integration (mrpt_ros2 packages). Active development since 2005 with one primary maintainer (Blanco-Claraco) is remarkable.
